concepts.title: Formation of the League of Nations
concepts.description: The League of Nations was formed after World War I with the aim of promoting international cooperation and preventing future wars, but it failed to prevent World War II.

concepts.title: Key features of the United Nations
concepts.description: The United Nations is a universal international organization with 193 member states, headquartered in New York, with the main objectives of maintaining international peace and security, promoting sustainable development, protecting human rights, delivering humanitarian aid, and upholding international law.

concepts.title: Role of the International Monetary Fund (IMF)
concepts.description: The IMF is an international organization that provides financial and technical assistance to its member countries to promote global economic stability and prosperity, by providing loans, policy advice, and technical assistance.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for the main organs of the United Nations
concepts.description: General Assembly (GA), Security Council (SC), Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC), Trusteeship Council (TC), International Court of Justice (ICJ), Secretariat (S)

concepts.title: Distinguishing between the League of Nations and the United Nations
concepts.description: Students often confuse the League of Nations with the United Nations, but while the League of Nations was formed after World War I and failed to prevent World War II, the United Nations was formed after World War II and has been successful in maintaining international peace and security.

concepts.title: Major International Organizations
concepts.description: United Nations, International Monetary Fund (IMF), World Bank, World Health Organization (WHO), International Labour Organization (ILO), World Trade Organization (WTO)

concepts.title: Purpose of the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
concepts.description: The UNDP is the United Nations' global development network, which aims to eradicate poverty and reduce inequalities through sustainable development, by providing technical assistance, policy advice, and funding to developing countries.

concepts.title: Key functions of the World Health Organization (WHO)
concepts.description: The WHO is responsible for setting global health policies, providing technical assistance, and coordinating international health efforts to promote health, keep the world safe, and serve the vulnerable.
